title: search_notes
description: Primary retrieval entry point for the query loop. Runs hybrid (vector + BM25 + graph boost) retrieval over the chunk index and returns ~top_k candidates with metadata (note_path, title, heading_path, chunk text, score, why-matched tag). The orchestrator typically follows this with rerank_results before sending context to Gemma. Result shape (not modeled here for v1) is an array of {chunk_id, note_path, title, heading_path, text, score, signal: 'semantic'|'lexical'|'backlink'|'tag'|'recency_boost'}.

query.description: The user's question or a paraphrase. For natural-language questions pass the raw user query; for keyword lookups extract the salient phrase. Must be non-empty.

mode.description: Retrieval mode. 'hybrid' (default choice): vector + BM25 + graph boosts, the right answer for almost every query. 'semantic': vector-only, useful for paraphrased lookups when filenames don't matter. 'lexical': BM25-only, for keyword-heavy queries. 'literal': exact-substring lookup, for quoted phrases, exact filenames, or exact tags — skips reranking entirely.

top_k.description: How many candidates to return BEFORE reranking. Use 30 for a typical hybrid run that will be reranked down to 8. Use 8 directly for 'literal' mode where reranking is skipped.
